children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.heading: Alternative fuel
children.label: 2 Alternative fuel
children.text: The term “alternative fuel” means liquefied natural gas, compressed natural gas, hydrogen, propane, or biofuels.

children.label: A
children.text: the Administrator certifies reduces emissions and is operated entirely or in part using an alternative fuel; or

children.label: B
children.text: is a zero-emission school bus.

children.label: A
children.text: to sell, lease, license, or contract for service clean school buses, zero-emission school buses, charging or fueling infrastructure, or other equipment needed to charge, fuel, or maintain clean school buses or zero-emission school buses, to individuals or entities that own, lease, license, or contract for service a school bus or a fleet of school buses; or

children.label: B
children.text: to arrange financing for such a sale, lease, license, or contract for service.

children.label: I
children.text: providing school bus service to 1 or more public school systems; or

children.label: II
children.text: the purchase, lease, license, or contract for service of school buses;

children.label: ii
children.text: an eligible contractor;

children.label: iii
children.text: a nonprofit school transportation association;

children.label: iv
children.text: a charter school (as defined in ) responsible for the purchase, lease, license, or contract for service of school buses for that charter school; orsection 7221i of title 20

children.label: I
children.text: providing school bus service to 1 or more Bureau-funded schools (as defined in ); orsection 2021 of title 25

children.label: II
children.text: the purchase, lease, license, or contract for service of school buses.

children.heading: Special requirements
children.label: B Special requirements
children.text: In the case of eligible recipients identified under clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A), the Administrator shall establish timely and appropriate requirements for notice and shall establish timely and appropriate requirements for approval by the public school systems that would be served by buses purchased using award funds made available under this section.

children.heading: High-need local educational agency
children.label: 6 High-need local educational agency
children.text: The term “high-need local educational agency” means a local educational agency (as defined in ) that is among the local educational agencies in the applicable State with high percentages of children counted under , on the basis of the most recent satisfactory data available, as determined by the Secretary of Education (or, for a local educational agency for which no such data is available, such other data as the Secretary of Education determines to be satisfactory).section 7801 of title 20section 6333(c) of title 20

children.heading: School bus
children.label: 7 School bus
children.text: The term “school bus” has the meaning given the term “schoolbus” in .section 30125(a) of title 49

children.label: A
children.text: any air pollutant that is listed pursuant to (or any precursor to such an air pollutant); andsection 7408(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any greenhouse gas.

children.heading: Program for replacement of existing school buses with clean school buses and zero-emission school buses
children.label: b Program for replacement of existing school buses with clean school buses and zero-emission school buses
A
children.text: to award grants and rebates on a competitive basis to eligible recipients for the replacement of existing school buses with clean school buses;

children.label: B
children.text: to award grants and rebates on a competitive basis to eligible recipients for the replacement of existing school buses with zero-emission school buses;

children.label: C
children.text: to award contracts to eligible contractors to provide rebates for the replacement of existing school buses with clean school buses; and

children.label: D
children.text: to award contracts to eligible contractors to provide rebates for the replacement of existing school buses with zero-emission school buses.

children.label: A
children.text: 50 percent to replace existing school buses with zero-emission school buses; and

children.label: B
children.text: 50 percent to replace existing school buses with clean school buses and zero-emission school buses.

children.label: A
children.text: Lowest overall cost of bus replacement.

children.label: B
children.text: Local conditions, including the length of bus routes and weather conditions.

children.label: C
children.text: Technologies that most reduce emissions.

children.label: D
children.text: Whether funds will bring new technologies to scale or promote cost parity between old technology and new technology.

children.label: i
children.text: a high-need local educational agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: a Bureau-funded school (as defined in ); orsection 2021 of title 25

children.label: iii
children.text: a local educational agency that receives a basic support payment under for children who reside on Indian land;section 7703(b)(1) of title 20

children.label: B
children.text: serve rural or low-income areas; or

children.label: i
children.text: public-private partnerships;

children.label: ii
children.text: grants from other entities; or

children.label: iii
children.text: issuance of school bonds.

children.label: A
children.text: be operated as part of the school bus fleet for which the award was made for not less than 5 years, except that, if the award is to an eligible contractor and the contract with the local educational agency (including charter schools operating as local educational agencies under State law) ends before the end of the 5-year period, those school buses may be operated as part of another local educational agency eligible for the same or higher priority consideration under paragraph (4), subject to the limitations under paragraph (7);

children.label: B
children.text: be maintained, operated, and charged or fueled according to manufacturer recommendations or State requirements; and

children.label: C
children.text: not be manufactured or retrofitted with, or otherwise have installed, a power unit or other technology that creates air pollution within the school bus, such as an unvented diesel passenger heater.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In making awards under paragraph (1), the Administrator may make awards for up to 100 percent of the costs for replacement of existing school buses with clean school buses, zero-emission school buses, and charging or fueling infrastructure.

children.heading: Structuring awards
children.label: B Structuring awards
children.text: In making an award under paragraph (1)(A), the Administrator shall decide whether to award a grant or rebate, or a combination thereof, based primarily on how best to facilitate replacing existing school buses with clean school buses or zero-emission school buses, as applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, achieve nationwide deployment of clean school buses and zero-emission school buses through the program under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure a broad geographic distribution of awards.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Administrator shall ensure that the amount received by all eligible entities in a State from grants and rebates under this section does not exceed 10 percent of the amounts made available to carry out this section during a fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of applications received;

children.label: B
children.text: the quantity and amount of grants and rebates awarded and the location of the recipients of the grants and rebates;

children.label: C
children.text: the criteria used to select the recipients; and

children.label: D
children.text: any other information the Administrator considers appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Administrator shall develop an education and outreach program to promote and explain the award program under this section.November 15, 2021

children.heading: Coordination with stakeholders
children.label: 2 Coordination with stakeholders
children.text: The education and outreach program under paragraph (1) shall be designed and conducted in conjunction with interested stakeholders.

children.label: A
children.text: inform potential award recipients on the process of applying for awards and fulfilling the requirements of awards;

children.label: B
children.text: describe the available technologies and the benefits of using the technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: explain the benefits and costs incurred by participating in the award program;

children.label: i
children.text: clean school bus and zero-emission school bus acquisition and deployment;

children.label: ii
children.text: the build-out of associated infrastructure and advance planning with the local electricity supplier;

children.label: iii
children.text: workforce development, training, and Registered Apprenticeships that meet the requirements under parts 29 and 30 of title 29, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ); andDecember 1, 2019

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information that is necessary, as determined by the Administrator; and

children.label: E
children.text: include, as appropriate, information from the annual report required under subsection (b)(7).1So in original. Probably should be “subsection (b)(8).”1

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: d Administrative costs
children.text: The Administrator may use, for the administrative costs of carrying out this section, not more than 3 percent of the amounts made available to carry out this section for any fiscal year.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: The Administrator shall have the authority to issue such regulations or other guidance, forms, instructions, and publications as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the programs, projects, or activities authorized under this section, including to ensure that such programs, projects, or activities are completed in a timely and effective manner, result in emissions reductions, and maximize public health benefits.
$500,000,000 shall be made available for the adoption of clean school buses and zero-emission school buses; and
$500,000,000 shall be made available for the adoption of zero-emission school buses.
